Title: Transgenic expression of plastidic glutamine synthetase increases nitrogen uptake and yield in wheat

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glutamine synthetase GSII family; plastid-targeted isoform GS2
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Primary ammonium assimilation in plastids; photorespiratory nitrogen cycling
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream; first step of inorganic nitrogen assimilation into organic form
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: ATP-dependent assimilation of ammonium into glutamate to form glutamine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: grain nitrogen / grain protein (total amino acids)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: nitrogenous nutrients / amino acids / protein-related trait
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves grain nutritional quality and supports yield formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher total TaGS2 transcript in flag leaves at 14 DPA of transgenic lines; also elevated in shoots and roots under low N and high N (supplementary)
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: TaGS2-2Ab native promoter (TaGS2-2Abpro), inducibility not established
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low N and high N field conditions; seedling hydroponics with 0.2 mM or 2.0 mM nitrate; grain filling stage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression driven by native promoter in wheat; in vitro enzyme assay of recombinant proteins; qRT-PCR; Western blot; GS activity assay; field phenotyping under low/high N; 15N labeling; nitrate flux measurement; metabolite/amino acid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes nitrogen uptake, nitrogen remobilization to grain, higher grain nitrogen concentration and amino acids, prolonged flag-leaf function, and increased grain yield under both low and high nitrogen
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for nitrogen assimilation; paper supports physiological importance but does not formally establish as sole rate-limiting step
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaGS2-2A/2Ab encoded a higher-activity GS2 enzyme than TaGS2-2B and TaGS2-2Db in vitro. 2. Native-promoter transgenic expression of TaGS2-2Ab increased TaGS2 transcript, GS2 protein abundance, and GS activity in wheat flag leaves. 3. Transgenic lines showed increased N uptake before and after flowering, greater aerial N accumulation, higher grain N concentration, higher total amino acids, and increased N harvest index. 4. Grain yield, spike number, grain number per spike, and 1000-grain weight increased under both low-N and high-N field conditions. 5. Root nitrate influx and expression of nitrate transporters were elevated, and leaf functional duration/photosynthesis were prolonged.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excessively high leaf GS activity caused negative effects on grain yield, excessive stay-green, and reduced 1000-grain weight; possible metabolic imbalance from overexpression if not properly tuned
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for improving nitrogen use efficiency, grain yield, and grain protein concentration in wheat; recommended use with its native promoter and optimized expression level; useful for transgenic engineering and marker-assisted selection of favorable haplotypes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The favorable haplotype is TaGS2-2Ab from the TaGS2-2A locus. The paper distinguishes it from TaGS2-2B and TaGS2-2Db haplotypes/proteins. Main metabolite consequence is broad nitrogenous nutrient accumulation rather than a single small molecule.
